regimens.notes: Dexrazoxane may be added as cardioprotectant for patients planning to receive high-dose anthracyclines (>250 mg/m2 cumulative). Anthracycline-based regimens are preferred in the neoadjuvant and adjuvant settings. Referenced by refs 1-4 [1-4].

In the Italian randomized cooperative study, adjuvant chemotherapy with epirubicin and ifosfamide versus observation resulted in significantly better median DFS (48 vs 16 months) and median OS (75 vs 46 months). After 90 months follow-up, estimated 5-year OS rate was 66% vs 46% (P=.04). Referenced by ref 5 [5].

regimens.regimen_name: Neoadjuvant Pembrolizumab with RT followed by Adjuvant Pembrolizumab
regimens.site: Soft tissue sarcoma (UPS-related: limb girdle/extremity UPS/myxofibrosarcoma/dedifferentiated liposarcoma/pleomorphic liposarcoma)

drugs.name: Pembrolizumab
drugs.schedule: Every 3 weeks with RT, then adjuvant 400 mg every 6 weeks

regimens.notes: New regimen added in Version 4.2026. For UPS-related sarcomas only (limb girdle/extremity UPS/myxofibrosarcoma [MFS]/dedifferentiated liposarcoma [ddLPS]/pleomorphic liposarcoma). Pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV pembrolizumab. Referenced by ref 6 [6].
Recommended for leiomyosarcoma (LMS), or if ifosfamide is not considered appropriate. Referenced by refs 1, 2, 11, 12 [1, 2, 11, 12].
Single-agent doxorubicin for neoadjuvant/adjuvant setting. Referenced by refs 1, 2, 7, 8 [1, 2, 7, 8].
Useful in certain circumstances for neoadjuvant/adjuvant therapy. Referenced by refs 5, 8, 28 [5, 8, 28].

In the ISG-STS 1001 trial comparing standard neoadjuvant chemotherapy (epirubicin/ifosfamide) with histotype-specific regimens, at 46 months DFS was 62% for standard chemotherapy vs 38% for histotype-tailored regimens (HR 2.00; 95% CI 1.22-3.26; P=.006). Useful in certain circumstances for myxoid liposarcoma. Referenced by ref 32 [32].
Category 2B recommendation. Useful in certain circumstances for neoadjuvant/adjuvant therapy. Referenced by refs 25, 26 [25, 26].

Single-agent doxorubicin is an anthracycline-based preferred regimen for first-line therapy. A randomized controlled phase 3 trial examined intensified doxorubicin plus ifosfamide vs doxorubicin alone; no OS benefit for combination (14.3 vs 12.8 months; P=.076), but improved response rates (26% vs 14%; P=.0006) and PFS (7.4 vs 4.6 months; P=.003). Referenced by refs 1, 2, 7, 8 [1, 2, 7, 8].
Anthracycline-based preferred regimen for first-line therapy. Referenced by ref 9 [9].

Pegylated liposomal doxorubicin had equivalent activity and improved toxicity profile compared to doxorubicin; response rates were 9% and 10% for doxorubicin and liposomal doxorubicin, respectively. Referenced by ref 10 [10].
Phase III comparison of doxorubicin and dacarbazine given by bolus versus infusion. Referenced by refs 1, 2, 11-13 [1, 2, 11, 12, 13].

Intensified doxorubicin plus ifosfamide showed improved PFS (7.4 vs 4.6 months; P=.003) and response rates (26% vs 14%; P=.0006) versus single-agent doxorubicin. No OS benefit for combination (14.3 vs 12.8 months; P=.076). In the RTOG 9514 study of 66 patients with large high-grade STS treated with neoadjuvant MAID (mesna/doxorubicin/ifosfamide/dacarbazine) chemoradiation followed by surgery and adjuvant MAID, 5-year DFS, distant DFS, and OS rates were 56%, 64%, and 71%, respectively. Referenced by refs 1-4, 7 [1, 2, 3, 4, 7].
Anthracycline-based preferred regimen for first-line therapy. Referenced by ref 5 [5].

drugs.name: Trabectedin
drugs.schedule: 24-hour infusion on day 1, every 3 weeks (after doxorubicin cycle), then trabectedin monotherapy 1.5 mg/m2 every 3 weeks

regimens.notes: Doxorubicin alone versus doxorubicin with trabectedin followed by trabectedin alone as first-line therapy for metastatic or unresectable leiomyosarcoma (LMS-04). Referenced by refs 14, 15 [14, 15].

regimens.site: Soft tissue sarcoma (any subtype with NTRK gene fusion)
regimens.notes: Not intended for neoadjuvant or adjuvant therapy of nonmetastatic disease. Not recommended for angiosarcoma or pleomorphic RMS. Larotrectinib is a tropomyosin receptor kinase (TRK) inhibitor with demonstrated efficacy in NTRK fusion-positive tumors regardless of histology. Referenced by ref 16 [16].

regimens.site: Soft tissue sarcoma (any subtype with NTRK gene fusion)
regimens.notes: Not intended for neoadjuvant or adjuvant therapy of nonmetastatic disease. Not recommended for angiosarcoma or pleomorphic RMS. Referenced by ref 17 [17].

regimens.site: Soft tissue sarcoma (any subtype with NTRK gene fusion)

drugs.name: Repotrectinib
drugs.dose: 160 mg daily for 14 days, then 120 mg

regimens.notes: Not intended for neoadjuvant or adjuvant therapy of nonmetastatic disease. Not recommended for angiosarcoma or pleomorphic RMS. Referenced by ref 18 [18].
Other recommended option for first-line therapy. Referenced by refs listed in table [ref].

Category 2B. In the randomized phase II study (SARC 002), the combination of gemcitabine and docetaxel was associated with superior PFS (6.2 and 3.0 months, respectively) and OS (17.9 and 11.5 months, respectively) compared to gemcitabine alone. However, gemcitabine/docetaxel was not superior to single-agent doxorubicin in the GeDDiS phase III trial (PFS 23.7 vs 23.3 weeks; P=.06). Referenced by refs 25, 26 [25, 26].

For patients ineligible for IV systemic therapy or patients who are not candidates for anthracycline-based regimens. In the EPAZ study, pazopanib demonstrated non-inferior PFS compared with doxorubicin (4.4 vs 5.3 months) as first-line treatment in older patients with advanced/metastatic STS. Referenced by ref 281 [281].

regimens.site: Soft tissue sarcoma (any subtype with RET gene fusion)

drugs.name: Selpercatinib
drugs.dose: 160 mg (body weight <50 kg) or 320 mg (>=50 kg)

regimens.notes: For RET gene fusion-positive tumors regardless of soft tissue sarcoma subtype. Referenced by ref 33 [33].

Category 2B. In the GEIS randomized phase II study, gemcitabine plus dacarbazine resulted in superior PFS (4.2 vs 2 months; P=.005), OS (16.8 vs 8.2 months; P=.014), and ORR (49% vs 25%; P=.009) compared to dacarbazine alone. Referenced by ref 27 [27].

For patients who had failed at least one anthracycline-based chemotherapy regimen. Pazopanib significantly prolonged median PFS (4.6 vs 1.6 months for placebo; P<.0001) and there was a trend toward improved OS (12.5 and 11 months, respectively; P=.25), although not statistically significant. Optimal duration of TKI therapy has not been established. Discontinuation of TKI therapy can be considered in patients with stable disease. Referenced by refs 19, 135 [19, 135].

regimens.site: Soft tissue sarcoma (LPS: category 1; other subtypes: category 2A)
regimens.notes: Category 1 recommendation for liposarcoma, category 2A for other subtypes. In the phase 3 trial comparing eribulin and dacarbazine in 452 patients with advanced LMS or LPS, median OS was 13.5 months vs 11.5 months (HR 0.77; 95% CI 0.62-0.95; P=.017). Subgroup analysis demonstrated the survival benefit was limited to LPS. Referenced by refs 20, 21 [20, 21].

regimens.site: Soft tissue sarcoma (LPS and LMS: category 1; other subtypes: category 2A)
regimens.notes: Category 1 for liposarcoma and leiomyosarcoma (L-type), category 2A for non-L-type sarcomas. The phase 3 trial revealed a 2.7-month PFS benefit versus dacarbazine in metastatic LPS or LMS that progressed after anthracycline-based therapy. Another study supported efficacy in translocation-related sarcoma. Referenced by refs 22-24 [22, 23, 24].
Preferred option for subsequent lines. Referenced by refs 25, 26 [25, 26].

regimens.regimen_name: Repotrectinib (Subsequent-Line, NTRK Fusion-Positive, Not Previously Given)
regimens.site: Soft tissue sarcoma (any subtype with NTRK gene fusion)

drugs.name: Repotrectinib
drugs.dose: 160 mg daily for 14 days, then 120 mg

regimens.notes: Only for subsequent lines if not previously given. Referenced by ref 18 [18].
Other recommended option. Recommended only for palliative therapy. Referenced by refs 22, 27 [22, 27].
Other recommended option. Referenced by refs 5, 8, 28 [5, 8, 28].

trial_data.trial_name: Spanish Group for Research on Sarcomas

regimens.notes: In a phase II study, temozolomide resulted in an ORR of 15.5% with a median OS of 8 months in patients with advanced pretreated STS. The PFS rates at 3 months and 6 months were 39.5% and 26%, respectively. Recommended only for palliative therapy. Referenced by refs 29 [29].
In a retrospective study, vinorelbine induced overall response in 6% of patients and 26% had stable disease. Recommended only for palliative therapy. Referenced by refs 30 [30].

In the REGOSARC trial (n=182), compared to placebo, regorafenib significantly extended PFS in all cohorts except LPS. In patients with non-adipocytic STS, overall PFS for regorafenib and placebo was 4 months vs 1 month (HR 0.36; P<.0001). Recommended only for palliative therapy. Referenced by ref 31 [31].
Other recommended option. Referenced by refs listed in table [ref].
Other recommended option. Referenced by ref 27 [27].
Category 2B. Useful in certain circumstances. Referenced by ref 34 [34].
Category 2B. Useful in certain circumstances. Referenced by ref 35 [35].

regimens.site: Soft tissue sarcoma (MFS, UPS, dedifferentiated LPS, cutaneous angiosarcoma, undifferentiated sarcomas)

drugs.name: Pembrolizumab
drugs.schedule: Every 3 weeks, or 400 mg every 6 weeks

regimens.notes: Useful in certain circumstances. Multicenter phase II study of pembrolizumab in advanced soft tissue sarcoma and bone sarcomas (SARC028). Pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV pembrolizumab. Referenced by refs 36, 37 [36, 37].

regimens.site: Soft tissue sarcoma (MFS, UPS, dedifferentiated LPS, cutaneous angiosarcoma, undifferentiated sarcomas)

drugs.name: Nivolumab
drugs.schedule: Every 2 weeks (monotherapy), or 3 mg/kg with ipilimumab every 3 weeks x4, then nivolumab 240 mg every 2 weeks

drugs.name: Ipilimumab
drugs.schedule: Every 3 weeks x4 (when combined with nivolumab)

regimens.notes: Useful in certain circumstances. Nivolumab and hyaluronidase-nvhy is not approved for concurrent use with IV ipilimumab; however, for nivolumab monotherapy, nivolumab and hyaluronidase-nvhy subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV nivolumab. Referenced by refs 38-41 [38, 39, 40, 41].

regimens.site: Soft tissue sarcoma (any subtype with TMB-H)

drugs.name: Pembrolizumab
drugs.schedule: Every 3 weeks, or 400 mg every 6 weeks

biomarkers.requirement: >=10 mut/Mb as determined by FDA-approved test

regimens.notes: For the treatment of patients with unresectable or metastatic TMB-H (>=10 mut/Mb) tumors, as determined by an FDA-approved test, that have progressed following prior treatment and who have no satisfactory alternative treatment options. Referenced by refs 36, 42 [36, 42].

regimens.site: Soft tissue sarcoma (any subtype with MSI-H or dMMR)

drugs.name: Pembrolizumab
drugs.schedule: Every 3 weeks, or 400 mg every 6 weeks

biomarkers.requirement: Positive, as determined by FDA-approved test

regimens.notes: For the treatment of unresectable or metastatic MSI-H or mismatch repair deficient (dMMR) solid tumors as determined by an FDA-approved test, that have progressed following prior treatment and who have no satisfactory alternative treatment options. Referenced by ref 42 [42].
Category 2B. Useful in certain circumstances. Clinical activity of single-agent cabozantinib in patients with refractory soft-tissue sarcomas was demonstrated. Referenced by ref 43 [43].
For synovial sarcomas only. Patients must be HLA-A*02:01P, HLA-A*02:02P, HLA-A*02:03P or HLA-A*02:06P positive and whose tumor expresses the MAGE-A4 antigen. Referenced by ref 44 [44].
Gamma-secretase inhibitor. Category 1 preferred agent for desmoid tumors. Referenced by ref 45 [45].

Category 1 preferred agent for desmoid tumors. In a study of 26 patients (11 first-line, 15 had received a median of 2 prior systemic therapies), sorafenib induced PR in 25% of patients and 70% had stable disease, with a median follow-up of 6 months. Referenced by ref 46 [46].
Preferred option for desmoid tumors. Referenced by ref 47 [47].
Low-dose chemotherapy with methotrexate and vinblastine was associated with prolonged stable disease in patients with unresectable or recurrent tumors. Referenced by ref 48 [48].

In a phase II multicenter study (SARC 006), imatinib resulted in an objective response rate of 6% and the 1-year PFS rate was 66%. Long-term follow-up showed 2-year PFS and OS rates were 55% and 95%, respectively. Referenced by refs 49, 50 [49, 50].
Pegylated liposomal doxorubicin was an effective and well-tolerated treatment for refractory aggressive fibromatosis. Referenced by ref 51 [51].
Doxorubicin-based chemotherapy has been effective in patients with recurrent or unresectable tumors. Referenced by refs 52-54 [52, 53, 54].
Erratum in Version 4.2026: Revised regimen from dacarbazine +/- doxorubicin to Doxorubicin and Dacarbazine/Doxorubicin. Referenced by refs 52-54 [52, 53, 54].
In the DESMOPAZ study, pazopanib or methotrexate-vinblastine combination chemotherapy was evaluated in adult patients with progressive desmoid tumors. Referenced by ref 55 [55].

regimens.regimen_name: Sulindac or Other NSAIDs including Celecoxib (Desmoid Tumors)
regimens.notes: Useful in certain circumstances. For pain management. The risk of cardiovascular events may be increased in patients receiving celecoxib, and patients with cardiovascular disease or risk factors may be at greater risk. Referenced by ref 56 [56].

drugs.name: Vincristine
drugs.schedule: Day 1, weekly during induction, then every 3 weeks during maintenance

regimens.notes: Standard chemotherapy for pediatric nonmetastatic intermediate or high-risk RMS. In COG D9803, no significant survival benefit of adding topotecan to standard VAC in children with intermediate-risk RMS (4-year FFS 73% for VAC vs 68% for VAC/VTC; P=.30). Referral to institutions with expertise in RMS is strongly recommended. Referenced by ref 57 [57].

trial_data.trial_name: European Paediatric Soft Tissue Sarcoma Study Group MTS 2008

regimens.notes: Preferred option from European protocols. Results from the European Paediatric Soft Tissue Sarcoma Study Group MTS 2008 study and pooled analysis with the concurrent BERNIE study. Referenced by ref 58 [58].
Alternating VAC and IE regimens were found to be effective for intermediate-risk RMS. Referenced by ref 59 [59].
Other recommended regimen. Referenced by ref 60 [60].

Chemotherapy with vincristine, doxorubicin, and ifosfamide resulted in an overall response rate of 86% in 11 adult patients with pleomorphic RMS; 2-year OS and DFS rates were 55% and 64%, respectively. Referenced by ref 61 [61].
Other recommended regimen. Referenced by ref 62 [62].
Other recommended regimen. Referenced by ref 63 [63].
Ifosfamide and etoposide were found to be superior to vincristine and melphalan for pediatric metastatic RMS. Referenced by ref 64 [64].
Two consecutive phase II window trials of irinotecan alone or in combination with vincristine for the treatment of metastatic RMS. Referenced by refs 65, 66 [65, 66].
Report of the German CESS/CWS REZ 91 trial for treatment of children with relapsed soft tissue sarcoma. Referenced by ref 67 [67].

Maintenance chemotherapy for patients with intermediate-risk RMS with complete response (CR) following treatment with VAC or VAI regimen. The COG has an active prospective ongoing study, but this is considered a reasonable standard of care. In the RMS 2005 trial, maintenance therapy with vinorelbine and cyclophosphamide showed benefit. Referenced by ref 68 [68].
Extended phase I evaluation in children with refractory solid tumors. Referenced by ref 69 [69].
Phase II trial of irinotecan in children with relapsed or refractory rhabdomyosarcoma. Referenced by refs 65, 66, 70 [65, 66, 70].
Up-front window trial of topotecan in previously untreated children and adolescents with metastatic rhabdomyosarcoma. Referenced by ref 71 [71].

drugs.name: Vinorelbine
drugs.schedule: Weekly or Days 1 and 8, every 3 weeks

regimens.notes: Vinorelbine in previously treated advanced childhood sarcomas showed evidence of activity in rhabdomyosarcoma. Referenced by ref 72 [72].
Randomized phase II trial of bevacizumab or temsirolimus in combination with chemotherapy for first relapse rhabdomyosarcoma. Referenced by ref 73 [73].

drugs.name: Atezolizumab
drugs.schedule: Every 2 weeks, or 1200 mg every 3 weeks, or 1680 mg every 4 weeks

regimens.notes: FDA approved for the treatment of adult and pediatric patients >=2 years of age with unresectable or metastatic ASPS. Atezolizumab and hyaluronidase-tqjs subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV atezolizumab. Referenced by refs 74, 75 [74, 75].
Preferred agent for advanced ASPS. Referenced by ref 76 [76].
Preferred agent for advanced ASPS. Pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV pembrolizumab. Referenced by ref 77 [77].

New regimen added in Version 4.2026. Axitinib plus pembrolizumab in patients with advanced sarcomas including alveolar soft-part sarcoma: a single-centre, single-arm, phase 2 trial. Referenced by ref 78 [78].

drugs.name: Sunitinib
drugs.schedule: Once daily, 4 weeks on / 2 weeks off

regimens.notes: Sunitinib in advanced ASPS showed evidence of a direct antitumor effect with confirmed responses and prolonged stable disease. Referenced by refs 79, 80 [79, 80].
Useful in certain circumstances. Referenced by ref 43 [43].
Phase II trial of weekly paclitaxel for unresectable angiosarcoma (ANGIOTAX Study). Referenced by refs 81, 82 [81, 82].
Other recommended agent for angiosarcoma. Referenced by ref 83 [83].
Useful in certain circumstances. Referenced by ref 84 [84].
Phase II study of sorafenib in patients with metastatic or recurrent sarcomas. Referenced by ref 85 [85].

drugs.name: Sunitinib
drugs.schedule: Once daily, 4 weeks on / 2 weeks off

regimens.notes: Multicenter phase II trial of sunitinib in the treatment of non-GIST sarcomas. Referenced by ref 86 [86].

An open-label, multicenter, phase II study of bevacizumab for the treatment of angiosarcoma and epithelioid hemangioendotheliomas. An FDA-approved biosimilar is an appropriate substitute. Referenced by ref 87 [87].

drugs.name: Nivolumab
drugs.schedule: Every 3 weeks x4, then nivolumab 240 mg every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: Useful in certain circumstances for angiosarcoma. Referenced by ref 38 [38].
For cutaneous angiosarcoma specifically. Pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV pembrolizumab. Referenced by refs 36, 37 [36, 37].

Patients with DFSP with fibrosarcomatous changes and/or malignant transformations should be treated according to the algorithms for other soft tissue sarcomas (EXTSARC-1 and SARC-G, 4 of 14). Imatinib is the preferred first-line agent. Referenced by ref 88 [88].
Anthracycline-based regimens for general soft tissue sarcoma are preferred for epithelioid sarcoma. Referenced in SARC-G, 1 of 14 [ref].
Extraskeletal osteosarcoma: outcomes and the role of chemotherapy. Platinum-based therapy (Cisplatin/Doxorubicin) is an alternative preferred option. Referenced by ref 89 [89].
Anthracycline-based regimens and gemcitabine-based regimens recommended for general soft tissue sarcoma are preferred for dedifferentiated chordoma. Referenced in SARC-G, 1 of 14 [ref].

regimens.site: Inflammatory myofibroblastic tumor (IMT) with ALK translocation
regimens.notes: ALK inhibitor. Referenced by ref 90 [90].

regimens.site: Inflammatory myofibroblastic tumor (IMT) with ALK translocation

trial_data.trial_name: ALTA trials (Camidge et al. 2018; Gettinger et al. 2016)

regimens.notes: ALK inhibitor. Brigatinib versus crizotinib in ALK-positive NSCLC; also activity and safety in other malignancies. Referenced by refs 91, 92 [91, 92].

regimens.site: Inflammatory myofibroblastic tumor (IMT) with ALK translocation
regimens.notes: Next-generation ALK inhibitor. Referenced by ref 93 [93].

regimens.site: Inflammatory myofibroblastic tumor (IMT) with ALK translocation
regimens.notes: Crizotinib in ALK-rearranged inflammatory myofibroblastic tumor demonstrated dramatic clinical response. Long-term effects in ALK-positive tumors excluding NSCLC also studied. Referenced by refs 94 [94].

regimens.site: Inflammatory myofibroblastic tumor (IMT) with ALK translocation
regimens.notes: Third-generation ALK inhibitor. Referenced by ref 95 [95].
nab-Sirolimus for patients with malignant PEComa. Long-term follow-up for duration of response from AMPECT trial. Referenced by refs 96, 97 [96, 97].

drugs.name: Sirolimus
drugs.schedule: Once daily, with therapeutic drug monitoring

trial_data.trial_name: Wagner et al. 2010; Bissler et al. 2008; Davies et al. 2011

regimens.notes: mTOR inhibitor. Clinical activity demonstrated in malignant PEComa and in angiomyolipoma in tuberous sclerosis complex or lymphangioleiomyomatosis. Referenced by refs 98-101 [98, 99, 100, 101].
mTOR inhibitor. Referenced by ref 102 [102].

trial_data.trial_name: Benson et al. 2014; Italiano et al. 2010

regimens.notes: mTOR inhibitor. Treatment with the mTOR inhibitor temsirolimus in patients with malignant PEComa. Referenced by refs 103, 104 [103, 104].

drugs.name: Sirolimus
drugs.schedule: Once daily, with therapeutic drug monitoring

trial_data.trial_name: Bissler et al. 2008; McCormack et al. 2011

regimens.notes: mTOR inhibitors for recurrent angiomyolipoma and lymphangioleiomyomatosis. Sirolimus for angiomyolipoma in tuberous sclerosis complex or lymphangioleiomyomatosis (NEJM 2008). Efficacy and safety of sirolimus in lymphangioleiomyomatosis (NEJM 2011). Referenced by refs 98-104 [98, 99, 100, 101, 102, 103, 104].

Activity of temozolomide and bevacizumab in the treatment of locally advanced, recurrent, and metastatic hemangiopericytoma and malignant solitary fibrous tumor. An FDA-approved biosimilar is an appropriate substitute for bevacizumab. Referenced by ref 105 [105].

drugs.name: Sunitinib
drugs.schedule: Once daily, 4 weeks on / 2 weeks off

regimens.notes: Sunitinib malate in solitary fibrous tumor showed activity. Referenced by ref 106 [106].
Subgroup analysis from a phase II study of the French Sarcoma Group. Referenced by ref 107 [107].
Efficacy and safety of pazopanib for recurrent or metastatic solitary fibrous tumor. Referenced by ref 108 [108].

regimens.site: Tenosynovial giant cell tumor / pigmented villonodular synovitis (TGCT/PVNS)

drugs.name: Pexidartinib
drugs.dose: 1200 mg twice daily for 2 weeks, then 800 mg twice daily

regimens.notes: Category 1 preferred agent. Pexidartinib versus placebo for advanced tenosynovial giant cell tumor (ENLIVEN): a randomized phase 3 trial. CSF1/CSF1R inhibitor. Referenced by ref 109 [109].

regimens.site: Tenosynovial giant cell tumor / pigmented villonodular synovitis (TGCT/PVNS)
regimens.notes: Category 1 preferred agent. Vimseltinib versus placebo for tenosynovial giant cell tumour (MOTION). CSF1R inhibitor. Referenced by ref 110 [110].

regimens.site: Tenosynovial giant cell tumor / pigmented villonodular synovitis (TGCT/PVNS)
regimens.notes: Efficacy of imatinib mesylate for the treatment of locally advanced and/or metastatic tenosynovial giant cell tumor/pigmented villonodular synovitis. Referenced by ref 111 [111].

regimens.site: Dedifferentiated liposarcoma (with or without concurrent well-differentiated liposarcoma)
regimens.notes: Anthracycline-based and gemcitabine-based regimens recommended for general soft tissue sarcoma are preferred. Referenced in SARC-G, 1 of 14 [ref].

regimens.site: Dedifferentiated liposarcoma (with or without concurrent well-differentiated liposarcoma)
regimens.notes: Other recommended. Category 1 for LPS. Referenced by refs 20, 21 [20, 21].

regimens.site: Dedifferentiated liposarcoma (with or without concurrent well-differentiated liposarcoma)
regimens.notes: Other recommended. Category 1 for LPS. Referenced by refs 22-24 [22, 23, 24].

drugs.name: Palbociclib
drugs.schedule: Once daily for 21 days, every 28 days

regimens.notes: CDK4/6 inhibitor. Single-agent therapy for unresectable WDLPS or dedifferentiated liposarcoma. Phase II trial of the CDK4 inhibitor PD0332991 in patients with advanced CDK4-amplified well-differentiated or dedifferentiated liposarcoma. Referenced by ref 113 [113].

regimens.site: Dedifferentiated liposarcoma (with or without concurrent well-differentiated liposarcoma)
regimens.notes: CDK4/6 inhibitor. Therapy-induced senescence contributes to the efficacy of abemaciclib in patients with dedifferentiated liposarcoma. Referenced by ref 114 [114].
Pazopanib for treatment of advanced extraskeletal myxoid chondrosarcoma: a multicentre, single-arm, phase 2 trial. Referenced by ref 115 [115].

drugs.name: Sunitinib
drugs.schedule: Once daily, 4 weeks on / 2 weeks off

regimens.notes: Activity of sunitinib in extraskeletal myxoid chondrosarcoma. Referenced by ref 116 [116].
Other recommended. Referenced by ref 43 [43].
Various soft tissue sarcoma regimens, pazopanib, sirolimus, paclitaxel, and trametinib are listed as treatment options. Referenced by ref 117 [117].
Other recommended. Referenced by ref 117 [117].

drugs.name: Sirolimus
drugs.schedule: Once daily, with therapeutic drug monitoring

regimens.notes: Activity of sirolimus in patients with progressive epithelioid hemangioendothelioma within the Italian Rare Cancer Network. Referenced by ref 118 [118].
Category 2B. Referenced by ref 117 [117].
A single-arm phase 2 trial of trametinib in patients with locally advanced or metastatic epithelioid hemangioendothelioma. Referenced by ref 119 [119].
